prompt: Customer and order exports generated with a non-default delimited profile still arrive comma-separated. Downstream imports also split display names and delivery notes when those values contain the profile's selected separator. The default comma profile looks normal.

repo_files.sql/render_customer_row.sql:
SELECT
    CAST(c.customer_id AS TEXT) || ',' ||
    CASE
        WHEN instr(COALESCE(c.display_name, ''), ',') > 0
          OR instr(COALESCE(c.display_name, ''), '"') > 0
          OR instr(COALESCE(c.display_name, ''), char(10)) > 0
          OR instr(COALESCE(c.display_name, ''), char(13)) > 0
        THEN '"' || replace(COALESCE(c.display_name, ''), '"', '""') || '"'
        ELSE COALESCE(c.display_name, '')
    END ||
    CASE p.record_separator
        WHEN 'CRLF' THEN char(13) || char(10)
        ELSE char(10)
    END AS rendered_row
FROM customer AS c
JOIN export_profile AS p
    ON p.profile_name = :profile_name
WHERE c.customer_id = :customer_id;
repo_files.sql/render_order_row.sql:
SELECT
    CAST(o.order_id AS TEXT) || ',' ||
    CASE
        WHEN instr(COALESCE(o.delivery_note, ''), ',') > 0
          OR instr(COALESCE(o.delivery_note, ''), '"') > 0
          OR instr(COALESCE(o.delivery_note, ''), char(10)) > 0
          OR instr(COALESCE(o.delivery_note, ''), char(13)) > 0
        THEN '"' || replace(COALESCE(o.delivery_note, ''), '"', '""') || '"'
        ELSE COALESCE(o.delivery_note, '')
    END ||
    CASE p.record_separator
        WHEN 'CRLF' THEN char(13) || char(10)
        ELSE char(10)
    END AS rendered_row
FROM fulfillment_order AS o
JOIN export_profile AS p
    ON p.profile_name = :profile_name
WHERE o.order_id = :order_id;
